title: Step 3: Click  in the confirmation window to delete the private endpoint.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view the private endpoints for an online archive, you must have
[Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>) access or higher to the project.

To edit or delete private endpoints for an online archive, you must have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access or higher to the project.

sections.heading: View List of Private Endpoints Using the UI
sections.body:
To view the list of private endpoints for the Online Archives:

Click Federated Database Instance / Online Archive for
a private endpoint for your federated database instance or online archive.

The page displays the private endpoints for your Online Archives. For
each private endpoint, you can see the following information:

| Column Name                   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ----------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cloud Provider                | The cloud provider (AWS (Amazon Web Services) or Azure (Microsoft Azure)) hosting the VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or Azure (Microsoft Azure) virtual network that Atlas uses for the private endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Region                        | The region where the cloud provider deploys the private endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Endpoint Status               | The status of the private endpoint connection. Possible options:Pending: The endpoint request is awaiting approval from Atlas or the set up process isn't complete.OK: The endpoint is active and functioning correctly.Failed: There was an error in setting up the endpoint and it is not operational. Click Edit to fix the problem.Deleting: Atlas is in the process of removing the endpoint. |
| VPC ID / Virtual Network Name | The unique identifier of either the AWS (Amazon Web Services) VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or Azure (Microsoft Azure) virtual network used for the private endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Description                   | The description you added when creating the private endpoint.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Actions                       | The actions you can take on the private endpoint. You can:Edit the Private Endpoint for Online ArchivesDelete a Private Endpoint for an Online Archive                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |

sections.heading: Retrieve Private Endpoint Using the API
sections.body:
To retrieve a private endpoint for an online archive through the
API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET` request to the
[privateNetworkSettings/endpointIds/](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupprivatenetworksettingendpointid) endpoint with
the ID of the private endpoint to retrieve. To learn more about the
syntax and options, see [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupprivatenetworksettingendpointid).

sections.heading: Retrieve All Private Endpoints Using the API
sections.body:
To retrieve all the private endpoints for the online archives using the
API (Application Programming Interface), send a `GET` request to the
[privateNetworkSettings/endpointIds](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupprivatenetworksettingendpointids) endpoint. To
learn more about the syntax and options, see [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupprivatenetworksettingendpointids).

sections.heading: Edit the Private Endpoint for Online Archives
sections.body:
MongoDB supports AWS (Amazon Web Services) and Azure (Microsoft Azure) private endpoints for your
Online Archives. You can edit the details associated with a private endpoint
for Online Archives from the Atlas UI and Atlas Administration API.> **Note:** You can edit the private endpoints for a dedicated cluster. To
> learn more, see [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint).**Api:**Atlas Administration APITo edit a private endpoint for an online archive through the Atlas Administration API,
send a `POST` request to the [privateNetworkSettings](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createdatafederationprivateendpoint) endpoint with
the unique ID of the private endpoint to edit. If you change the
comment associated with the specified endpoint, Atlas makes no
change to the endpoint ID list. If you change the comment
associated with the specified endpoint, Atlas updates the
`comment` value only in the endpoint ID list.To learn more about the syntax and options, see the [Atlas Administration API documentation](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createdatafederationprivateendpoint).**Ui:**Atlas UITo edit the details associated with a private endpoint from the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Step 3: Click  for the private endpoint that you wish to modify.
sections.body: To learn more about the fields you can edit, see [Procedure](oa-config-private-endpoint-procedure).

sections.heading: Step 4: Click  or  for the changes to take effect.
Delete a Private Endpoint for an Online Archive
sections.body:
You can delete a private endpoint for Online Archive from the
Atlas UI and Atlas Administration API.

> **Note:** You can delete the private endpoints for a dedicated cluster. To
> learn more, see [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint).

sections.heading: Delete Private Endpoint Through the User Interface
sections.body:
To delete a private endpoint from the Atlas UI:

Click Federated Database Instance / Online Archive
for a private endpoint for your federated database instance or online archive.

sections.heading: Delete a Private Endpoint Through the API
sections.body:
To delete a private endpoint through the API (Application Programming Interface), send a `DELETE`
request to the [privateNetworkSettings/endpointIds](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroupprivatenetworksettingendpointid) endpoint with the ID of the private endpoint to
delete. To learn more about the syntax and options, see
[API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroupprivatenetworksettingendpointid).
